title: viola alba subsp. dehnhardtii
intro: Viola alba subsp. dehnhardtii, also known as white violets, is a subspecies of the common violet plant native to Europe and Western Asia. It is a perennial herb with heart-shaped leaves and white, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, which make it useful for treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The leaves and flowers of Viola alba subsp. dehnhardtii can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, to promote health and well-being, with benefits including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system.
